events.content:
Build a toolbar with grouped buttons using Nuxt UI components.

Requirements:

1. Create a toolbar with 3 button groups:
   - **Text formatting group**: Bold, Italic, Underline buttons with appropriate Lucide icons
   - **Alignment group**: Left, Center, Right align buttons with icons
   - **Actions group**: A "Save" button with label + a dropdown chevron button
2. Group each set of related buttons together so they appear visually connected (no gaps between buttons in the same group)
3. Use `variant="outline"` and `color="neutral"` for all buttons
4. The groups should support different sizes — use `size="sm"` for the formatting group and default size for others
5. Add a vertical orientation group as well (e.g., a sidebar with stacked navigation buttons)

Focus on correct Nuxt UI v4 component usage for grouping buttons together.

message.content:
Build a toolbar with grouped buttons using Nuxt UI components.

Requirements:

1. Create a toolbar with 3 button groups:
   - **Text formatting group**: Bold, Italic, Underline buttons with appropriate Lucide icons
   - **Alignment group**: Left, Center, Right align buttons with icons
   - **Actions group**: A "Save" button with label + a dropdown chevron button
2. Group each set of related buttons together so they appear visually connected (no gaps between buttons in the same group)
3. Use `variant="outline"` and `color="neutral"` for all buttons
4. The groups should support different sizes — use `size="sm"` for the formatting group and default size for others
5. Add a vertical orientation group as well (e.g., a sidebar with stacked navigation buttons)

Focus on correct Nuxt UI v4 component usage for grouping buttons together.

tool.result:
2510	\`\`\`bash
2511	bun add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
2512	\`\`\`
2513	
2514	## Changes from v3
2515	
2516	After upgrading to Nuxt UI v4, please note the following important changes:
2517	
2518	### Renamed ButtonGroup
2519	
2520	The `ButtonGroup` component has been renamed to [`FieldGroup`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/field-group):
2521	
2522	```diff
2523	<template>
2524	- <UButtonGroup>
2525	+ <UFieldGroup>
2526	    <UButton label="Button" />
2527	    <UButton icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" />
2528	+ </UFieldGroup>
2529	- </UButtonGroup>
2530	</template>
2531	```
2532	
2533	### Renamed PageMarquee
2534	
2535	The `PageMarquee` component has been renamed to [`Marquee`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/marquee):
2536	
2537	```diff
2538	<template>
2539	- <UPageMarquee :items="items" />
2540	+ <UMarquee :items="items" />
2541	</template>
2542	```
2543	
2544	### Removed PageAccordion
2545	
2546	The `PageAccordion` component has been removed in favor of [`Accordion`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/accordion):
2547	
2548	```diff
2549	<template>
2550	- <UPageAccordion
2551	+ <UAccordion
2552	    :items="items"
2553	+   :unmount-on-hide="false"
2554	+   :ui="{ trigger: 'text-base', body: 'text-base text-muted' }"
2555	  />
2556	</template>
2557	```
2558	
2559	\> \[!NOTE]
2560	\> The \`PageAccordion\` component was a wrapper that set \`unmount-on-hide\` to \`false\` and customized the \`ui\` prop.
2561	
2562	### Renamed model modifiers
2563	
2564	The `modelModifiers` shape used by [`Input`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/input), [`InputNumber`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/input-number) and [`Textarea`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/textarea) has changed in v4:
2565	
2566	1. The `nullify` modifier was renamed to `nullable` (it converts empty/blank values to `null`).
2567	2. A new `optional` modifier was added (it converts empty/blank values to `undefined`).
2568	
2569	```diff
2570	- <UInput v-model.nullify="value" />
2571	+ <UInput v-model.nullable="value" />
2572	```
2573	
2574	```diff
2575	- <UTextarea v-model="value" :model-modifiers="{ nullify: true }" />
2576	+ <UTextarea v-model="value" :model-modifiers="{ nullable: true }" />
2577	```
2578	
2579	Use `nullable` when you want empty values as `null`, and `optional` when you prefer `undefined` for absent values.
2580	
2581	### Changes to Form component
2582	
2583	The `Form` component has been improved in v4 with better state management and nested form handling. Here are the key changes you need to be aware of:
2584	
2585	1. Schema **transformations will only** be applied to the **`@submit` data** and will no longer mutate the form's state. This provides better predictability and prevents unexpected state mutations.
2586	2. **Nested forms must be enabled explicitly** using the `nested` prop. This makes the component behavior more explicit and prevents accidental nested form creation.
2587	3. **Nested forms should now provide a `name`** prop (similar to `UFormField`) and will automatically inherit their state from their parent form.
2588	
2589	```diff

raw.content:
2510	\`\`\`bash
2511	bun add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
2512	\`\`\`
2513	
2514	## Changes from v3
2515	
2516	After upgrading to Nuxt UI v4, please note the following important changes:
2517	
2518	### Renamed ButtonGroup
2519	
2520	The `ButtonGroup` component has been renamed to [`FieldGroup`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/field-group):
2521	
2522	```diff
2523	<template>
2524	- <UButtonGroup>
2525	+ <UFieldGroup>
2526	    <UButton label="Button" />
2527	    <UButton icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" />
2528	+ </UFieldGroup>
2529	- </UButtonGroup>
2530	</template>
2531	```
2532	
2533	### Renamed PageMarquee
2534	
2535	The `PageMarquee` component has been renamed to [`Marquee`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/marquee):
2536	
2537	```diff
2538	<template>
2539	- <UPageMarquee :items="items" />
2540	+ <UMarquee :items="items" />
2541	</template>
2542	```
2543	
2544	### Removed PageAccordion
2545	
2546	The `PageAccordion` component has been removed in favor of [`Accordion`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/accordion):
2547	
2548	```diff
2549	<template>
2550	- <UPageAccordion
2551	+ <UAccordion
2552	    :items="items"
2553	+   :unmount-on-hide="false"
2554	+   :ui="{ trigger: 'text-base', body: 'text-base text-muted' }"
2555	  />
2556	</template>
2557	```
2558	
2559	\> \[!NOTE]
2560	\> The \`PageAccordion\` component was a wrapper that set \`unmount-on-hide\` to \`false\` and customized the \`ui\` prop.
2561	
2562	### Renamed model modifiers
2563	
2564	The `modelModifiers` shape used by [`Input`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/input), [`InputNumber`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/input-number) and [`Textarea`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/textarea) has changed in v4:
2565	
2566	1. The `nullify` modifier was renamed to `nullable` (it converts empty/blank values to `null`).
2567	2. A new `optional` modifier was added (it converts empty/blank values to `undefined`).
2568	
2569	```diff
2570	- <UInput v-model.nullify="value" />
2571	+ <UInput v-model.nullable="value" />
2572	```
2573	
2574	```diff
2575	- <UTextarea v-model="value" :model-modifiers="{ nullify: true }" />
2576	+ <UTextarea v-model="value" :model-modifiers="{ nullable: true }" />
2577	```
2578	
2579	Use `nullable` when you want empty values as `null`, and `optional` when you prefer `undefined` for absent values.
2580	
2581	### Changes to Form component
2582	
2583	The `Form` component has been improved in v4 with better state management and nested form handling. Here are the key changes you need to be aware of:
2584	
2585	1. Schema **transformations will only** be applied to the **`@submit` data** and will no longer mutate the form's state. This provides better predictability and prevents unexpected state mutations.
2586	2. **Nested forms must be enabled explicitly** using the `nested` prop. This makes the component behavior more explicit and prevents accidental nested form creation.
2587	3. **Nested forms should now provide a `name`** prop (similar to `UFormField`) and will automatically inherit their state from their parent form.
2588	
2589	```diff

tool.name: unknown
tool.result:
32432	# FieldGroup
32433	
32434	## Usage
32435	
32436	Wrap multiple [Button](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/button) within a FieldGroup to group them together.
32437	
32438	```vue
32439	<template>
32440	  <UFieldGroup>
32441	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Button" />
32442	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline" icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" />
32443	  </UFieldGroup>
32444	</template>
32445	```
32446	
32447	### Size
32448	
32449	Use the `size` prop to change the size of all the buttons.
32450	
32451	```vue
32452	<template>
32453	  <UFieldGroup size="xl">
32454	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Button" />
32455	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline" icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" />
32456	  </UFieldGroup>
32457	</template>
32458	```
32459	
32460	### Orientation
32461	
32462	Use the `orientation` prop to change the orientation of the buttons. Defaults to `horizontal`.
32463	
32464	```vue
32465	<template>
32466	  <UFieldGroup orientation="vertical">
32467	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Submit" />
32468	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline" label="Cancel" />
32469	  </UFieldGroup>
32470	</template>
32471	```
32472	
32473	## Examples
32474	
32475	### With input
32476	
32477	You can use components like [Input](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/input), [InputMenu](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/input-menu), [Select](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/select) [SelectMenu](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/select-menu), etc. within a field group.
32478	
32479	```vue
32480	<template>
32481	  <UFieldGroup>
32482	    <UInput color="neutral" variant="outline" placeholder="Enter token" />
32483	
32484	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" icon="i-lucide-clipboard" />
32485	  </UFieldGroup>
32486	</template>
32487	```
32488	
32489	### With tooltip
32490	
32491	You can use a [Tooltip](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/tooltip) within a field group.
32492	
32493	```vue [FieldGroupTooltipExample.vue]
32494	<template>
32495	  <UFieldGroup>
32496	    <UInput color="neutral" variant="outline" placeholder="Enter token" />
32497	
32498	    <UTooltip text="Copy to clipboard">
32499	      <UButton
32500	        color="neutral"
32501	        variant="subtle"
32502	        icon="i-lucide-clipboard"
32503	      />
32504	    </UTooltip>
32505	  </UFieldGroup>
32506	</template>
32507	```
32508	
32509	### With dropdown menu
32510	
32511	You can use a [DropdownMenu](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/dropdown-menu) within a field group.
32512	
32513	```vue [FieldGroupDropdownExample.vue]
32514	<script setup lang="ts">
32515	import type { DropdownMenuItem } from '@nuxt/ui'
32516	
32517	const items: DropdownMenuItem[] = [
32518	  {
32519	    label: 'Team',
32520	    icon: 'i-lucide-users'
32521	  },
32522	  {
32523	    label: 'Invite users',
32524	    icon: 'i-lucide-user-plus',
32525	    children: [
32526	      {
32527	        label: 'Invite by email',
32528	        icon: 'i-lucide-send-horizontal'
32529	      },
32530	      {
32531	        label: 'Invite by link',
32532	        icon: 'i-lucide-link'
32533	      }
32534	    ]
32535	  },
32536	  {
32537	    label: 'New team',
32538	    icon: 'i-lucide-plus'
32539	  }
32540	]
32541	</script>
32542	
32543	<template>
32544	  <UFieldGroup>
32545	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Settings" />
32546	
32547	    <UDropdownMenu :items="items">
32548	      <UButton
32549	        color="neutral"
32550	        variant="outline"
32551	        icon="i-lucide-chevron-down"
32552	      />
32553	    </UDropdownMenu>
32554	  </UFieldGroup>
32555	</template>
32556	```
32557	
32558	### With badge
32559	
32560	You can use a [Badge](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/badge) within a field group.
32561	
32562	```vue [FieldGroupBadgeExample.vue]
32563	<template>
32564	  <UFieldGroup>
32565	    <UBadge color="neutral" variant="outline" size="lg" label="https://" />
32566	
32567	    <UInput color="neutral" variant="outline" placeholder="www.example.com" />
32568	  </UFieldGroup>
32569	</template>
32570	```
32571	
32572	## API
32573	
32574	### Props
32575	
32576	```ts
32577	/**
32578	 * Props for the FieldGroup component
32579	 */
32580	interface FieldGroupProps {
32581	  /**
32582	   * The element or component this component should render as.
32583	   */
32584	  as?: any;
32585	  size?: "md" | "xs" | "sm" | "lg" | "xl" | undefined;
32586	  /**
32587	   * The orientation the buttons are laid out.
32588	   * @default "\"horizontal\""
32589	   */
32590	  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" | undefined;
32591	  ui?: { base?: any; } | undefined;
32592	}
32593	```
32594	
32595	### Slots
32596	
32597	```ts
32598	/**
32599	 * Slots for the FieldGroup component
32600	 */
32601	interface FieldGroupSlots {
32602	  default(): any;
32603	}
32604	```
32605	
32606	## Theme
32607	
32608	```ts [app.config.ts]
32609	export default defineAppConfig({
32610	  ui: {
32611	    fieldGroup: {
32612	      base: 'relative',
32613	      variants: {
32614	        size: {
32615	          xs: '',
32616	          sm: '',
32617	          md: '',
32618	          lg: '',
32619	          xl: ''
32620	        },
32621	        orientation: {
32622	          horizontal: 'inline-flex -space-x-px',
32623	          vertical: 'flex flex-col -space-y-px'
32624	        }
32625	      }
32626	    }
32627	  }
32628	})
32629	```
32630	
32631	## Changelog
32632	
32633	See commit history for \[component]\(https\://github.com/nuxt/ui/commits/v4/src/runtime/components/FieldGroup.vue) and \[theme]\(https\://github.com/nuxt/ui/commits/v4/src/theme/field-group.ts).
32634	
32635	
32636	# FileUpload
32637	
32638	## Usage
32639	
32640	Use the `v-model` directive to control the value of the FileUpload.
32641

raw.content:
32432	# FieldGroup
32433	
32434	## Usage
32435	
32436	Wrap multiple [Button](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/button) within a FieldGroup to group them together.
32437	
32438	```vue
32439	<template>
32440	  <UFieldGroup>
32441	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Button" />
32442	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline" icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" />
32443	  </UFieldGroup>
32444	</template>
32445	```
32446	
32447	### Size
32448	
32449	Use the `size` prop to change the size of all the buttons.
32450	
32451	```vue
32452	<template>
32453	  <UFieldGroup size="xl">
32454	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Button" />
32455	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline" icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" />
32456	  </UFieldGroup>
32457	</template>
32458	```
32459	
32460	### Orientation
32461	
32462	Use the `orientation` prop to change the orientation of the buttons. Defaults to `horizontal`.
32463	
32464	```vue
32465	<template>
32466	  <UFieldGroup orientation="vertical">
32467	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Submit" />
32468	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline" label="Cancel" />
32469	  </UFieldGroup>
32470	</template>
32471	```
32472	
32473	## Examples
32474	
32475	### With input
32476	
32477	You can use components like [Input](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/input), [InputMenu](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/input-menu), [Select](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/select) [SelectMenu](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/select-menu), etc. within a field group.
32478	
32479	```vue
32480	<template>
32481	  <UFieldGroup>
32482	    <UInput color="neutral" variant="outline" placeholder="Enter token" />
32483	
32484	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" icon="i-lucide-clipboard" />
32485	  </UFieldGroup>
32486	</template>
32487	```
32488	
32489	### With tooltip
32490	
32491	You can use a [Tooltip](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/tooltip) within a field group.
32492	
32493	```vue [FieldGroupTooltipExample.vue]
32494	<template>
32495	  <UFieldGroup>
32496	    <UInput color="neutral" variant="outline" placeholder="Enter token" />
32497	
32498	    <UTooltip text="Copy to clipboard">
32499	      <UButton
32500	        color="neutral"
32501	        variant="subtle"
32502	        icon="i-lucide-clipboard"
32503	      />
32504	    </UTooltip>
32505	  </UFieldGroup>
32506	</template>
32507	```
32508	
32509	### With dropdown menu
32510	
32511	You can use a [DropdownMenu](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/dropdown-menu) within a field group.
32512	
32513	```vue [FieldGroupDropdownExample.vue]
32514	<script setup lang="ts">
32515	import type { DropdownMenuItem } from '@nuxt/ui'
32516	
32517	const items: DropdownMenuItem[] = [
32518	  {
32519	    label: 'Team',
32520	    icon: 'i-lucide-users'
32521	  },
32522	  {
32523	    label: 'Invite users',
32524	    icon: 'i-lucide-user-plus',
32525	    children: [
32526	      {
32527	        label: 'Invite by email',
32528	        icon: 'i-lucide-send-horizontal'
32529	      },
32530	      {
32531	        label: 'Invite by link',
32532	        icon: 'i-lucide-link'
32533	      }
32534	    ]
32535	  },
32536	  {
32537	    label: 'New team',
32538	    icon: 'i-lucide-plus'
32539	  }
32540	]
32541	</script>
32542	
32543	<template>
32544	  <UFieldGroup>
32545	    <UButton color="neutral" variant="subtle" label="Settings" />
32546	
32547	    <UDropdownMenu :items="items">
32548	      <UButton
32549	        color="neutral"
32550	        variant="outline"
32551	        icon="i-lucide-chevron-down"
32552	      />
32553	    </UDropdownMenu>
32554	  </UFieldGroup>
32555	</template>
32556	```
32557	
32558	### With badge
32559	
32560	You can use a [Badge](https://ui.nuxt.com/components/badge) within a field group.
32561	
32562	```vue [FieldGroupBadgeExample.vue]
32563	<template>
32564	  <UFieldGroup>
32565	    <UBadge color="neutral" variant="outline" size="lg" label="https://" />
32566	
32567	    <UInput color="neutral" variant="outline" placeholder="www.example.com" />
32568	  </UFieldGroup>
32569	</template>
32570	```
32571	
32572	## API
32573	
32574	### Props
32575	
32576	```ts
32577	/**
32578	 * Props for the FieldGroup component
32579	 */
32580	interface FieldGroupProps {
32581	  /**
32582	   * The element or component this component should render as.
32583	   */
32584	  as?: any;
32585	  size?: "md" | "xs" | "sm" | "lg" | "xl" | undefined;
32586	  /**
32587	   * The orientation the buttons are laid out.
32588	   * @default "\"horizontal\""
32589	   */
32590	  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" | undefined;
32591	  ui?: { base?: any; } | undefined;
32592	}
32593	```
32594	
32595	### Slots
32596	
32597	```ts
32598	/**
32599	 * Slots for the FieldGroup component
32600	 */
32601	interface FieldGroupSlots {
32602	  default(): any;
32603	}
32604	```
32605	
32606	## Theme
32607	
32608	```ts [app.config.ts]
32609	export default defineAppConfig({
32610	  ui: {
32611	    fieldGroup: {
32612	      base: 'relative',
32613	      variants: {
32614	        size: {
32615	          xs: '',
32616	          sm: '',
32617	          md: '',
32618	          lg: '',
32619	          xl: ''
32620	        },
32621	        orientation: {
32622	          horizontal: 'inline-flex -space-x-px',
32623	          vertical: 'flex flex-col -space-y-px'
32624	        }
32625	      }
32626	    }
32627	  }
32628	})
32629	```
32630	
32631	## Changelog
32632	
32633	See commit history for \[component]\(https\://github.com/nuxt/ui/commits/v4/src/runtime/components/FieldGroup.vue) and \[theme]\(https\://github.com/nuxt/ui/commits/v4/src/theme/field-group.ts).
32634	
32635	
32636	# FileUpload
32637	
32638	## Usage
32639	
32640	Use the `v-model` directive to control the value of the FileUpload.

10696	# Button
10697	
10698	## Usage
10699	
10700	Use the default slot to set the label of the Button.
10701	
10702	```vue
10703	<template>
10704	  <UButton>
10705	    Button
10706	  </UButton>
10707	</template>
10708	```
10709	
10710	### Label
10711	
10712	Use the `label` prop to set the label of the Button.
10713	
10714	```vue
10715	<template>
10716	  <UButton label="Button" />
10717	</template>
10718	```
10719	
10720	### Color
10721	
10722	Use the `color` prop to change the color of the Button.
10723	
10724	```vue
10725	<template>
10726	  <UButton color="neutral">
10727	    Button
10728	  </UButton>
10729	</template>
10730	```
10731	
10732	### Variant
10733	
10734	Use the `variant` prop to change the variant of the Button.
10735	
10736	```vue
10737	<template>
10738	  <UButton color="neutral" variant="outline">
10739	    Button
10740	  </UButton>
10741	</template>
10742	```
10743	
10744	### Size
10745	
10746	Use the `size` prop to change the size of the Button.
10747	
10748	```vue
10749	<template>
10750	  <UButton size="xl">
10751	    Button
10752	  </UButton>
10753	</template>
10754	```
10755	
10756	### Icon
10757	
10758	Use the `icon` prop to show an [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon) inside the Button.
10759	
10760	```vue
10761	<template>
10762	  <UButton icon="i-lucide-rocket" size="md" color="primary" variant="solid">
10763	    Button
10764	  </UButton>
10765	</template>
10766	```
10767	
10768	Use the `leading` and `trailing` props to set the icon position or the `leading-icon` and `trailing-icon` props to set a different icon for each position.
10769	
10770	```vue
10771	<template>
10772	  <UButton trailing-icon="i-lucide-arrow-right" size="md">
10773	    Button
10774	  </UButton>
10775	</template>
10776	```
10777	
10778	The `label` as prop or slot is optional so you can use the Button as an icon-only button.
10779	
10780	```vue
10781	<template>
10782	  <UButton icon="i-lucide-search" size="md" color="primary" variant="solid" />
10783	</template>
10784	```
10785	
10786	### Avatar
10787	
10788	Use the `avatar` prop to show an [Avatar](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/avatar) inside the Button.
10789	
10790	```vue
10791	<template>
10792	  <UButton size="md" color="neutral" variant="outline">
10793	    Button
10794	  </UButton>
10795	</template>
